blogs.contentHtml:
The future of rich-text editing is here. We designed LexiBlog to give you unparalleled control over every single pixel of your content.


Check out what we've built, including native video embedding:


Of course, we still support classic typography features. Need to write out water? H2O. Need to write out Einstein's formula? E = mc2.


It just works.
blogs.metaDescription: A complete demonstration of the features available in LexiBlog 3.0, including rich-text editing, embedded media, and deep customization.
blogs.author: The LexiBlog Team
blogs.keywords: lexiblog, editor, rich text, demo, features

blogs.title: Mastering React Component Composition
blogs.content:

But there's a better way: ","type":"text","version":1},{"detail":0,"format":1,"mode":"normal","style":"color: #4F46E5;","text":"Component Composition.","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":null,"format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"function Card({ children }) {\n return
{children}
;\n}","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"code","version":1,"language":"javascript"},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":null,"format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Here is an excellent visualization of this concept:","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""},{"type":"youtube","version":1,"videoID":"3XaXKiXtNro"},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":null,"format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":2,"mode":"normal","style":"font-family: Menlo, Consolas, monospace; color: #8A7E71; font-size: 14px;","text":""Stop blindly passing props" - Dan Abramov","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"center","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"root","version":1}}
blogs.contentHtml:
When building large-scale applications, you'll inevitably run into the prop-drilling problem.


Often, new developers will try to fix this by throwing Redux at the problem immediately. But there's a better way: Component Composition.


function Card({ children }) { return <div className="card">{children}</div>; }


Here is an excellent visualization of this concept:


"Stop blindly passing props" - Dan Abramov
blogs.metaDescription: A comprehensive guide on leveraging React Component Composition to solve prop-drilling without adding complex external state managers like Redux.
blogs.author: David Chen
blogs.keywords: react, composition, prop drilling, architecture, frontend

blogs.title: Minimalism in UI Design

It should not scream for your attention, but quietly solve problems.","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":null,"format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""},{"children":[{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Soft Borders","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"listitem","version":1,"value":1},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Muted Colors","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"listitem","version":1,"value":2},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"High Legibility Typography ","type":"text","version":1},{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"background-color: #FDFCF8; color: #8E8A86;","text":"(Crucial Rule)","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"listitem","version":1,"value":3}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"list","version":1,"listType":"bullet","start":1,"tag":"ul"},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":null,"format":"","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"font-size: 30px; color: #D97706;","text":"The Golden Rule of UI","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"center","indent":0,"type":"heading","version":1,"tag":"h2"},{"children":[{"detail":0,"format":0,"mode":"normal","style":"","text":"Less is always more. For an in-depth dive watch this amazing breakdown:","type":"text","version":1}],"direction":"ltr","format":"center","indent":0,"type":"paragraph","version":1,"textFormat":0,"textStyle":""},{"type":"youtube","version":1,"videoID":"h1Yv_M_E_0U"}],"direction":"ltr","format":"","indent":0,"type":"root","version":1}}
blogs.contentHtml:
Design should be silent. It should not scream for your attention, but quietly solve problems.


  • Soft Borders
  • Muted Colors
  • High Legibility Typography (Crucial Rule)


The Golden Rule of UI

Less is always more. For an in-depth dive watch this amazing breakdown:
blogs.metaDescription: Explore the principles of minimalist UI layout architecture, focusing on muted color palettes, typography legibility, and visual hierarchy.
blogs.author: Elena Rodriguez
blogs.keywords: ui design, minimalism, graphic design, layout, typography
